name: The Beholden
concept: You were no one. A power lifted you out of the gutter, the ledger, or the grave, and made you useful. Now their reach is your reach, and their leash is always around your throat.
duty: Your power is borrowed. Everything you can do, you can do because someone above you allows it. For now.

look.label: Mark
options: a brand or tattoo, kept covered

gear.label: Tool of the trade
options: a walking cane that is also a club (d6)

gear.label: Insurance
options: a purse of someone else's coin
a single favor token from your patron

moves.name: Strings
moves.trigger: When you arrive within your patron's reach and quietly put out feelers for a contact.

results.success: You find someone who can help you here.
results.failure: No one useful surfaces, or the only one who does already answers to someone else.
advantage: Hold 1 String (spend later: a contact is already here / a nearby debt to call in / a back way or safe room / a token of passage, forged or genuine).
Learn who else has been asking after the same thing.
threat: Add 1 Heat as word of your asking travels.
The contact wants something up front before they help.
results.triumph: Someone with real reach takes a favorable interest in you.
results.despair: You have walked into a rival's net; they know you are here and why.

hold.name: Strings
spend: A useful contact is already here and willing to talk.
Someone nearby owes you a small debt you can call in.
You know a back way, a safe room, or a quiet exit.
You produce a token of passage, forged or genuine.

moves.name: Leash
moves.text: You may add 1 Debt to upgrade your pool once (one ability die becomes proficiency) or to add a boost die. The patron remembers every indulgence.

moves.name: Read the Room
moves.trigger: When you enter a charged situation and read it.

results.success: Ask the GM one: who holds the real power here? who is afraid? who owes whom? where is the quickest way out? Acting on the answer grants a boost die.
results.failure: You misjudge the room, and the GM tells you how it bites you.
advantage: Spot a second exit or a useful object in reach.
threat: Take a setback die on your next move in this scene.
results.triumph: You spot the single lever that turns the whole room.
results.despair: You badly misread someone dangerous, to your cost.

moves.name: A Face for Every Door
moves.trigger: When you pass yourself off as someone you are not.

advantage: They buy it until you give them a hard reason not to.
You glean something only the role would be told.
threat: The act costs you: drop a coin, a possession, or a name.
results.triumph: They take you for someone important, and doors open accordingly.
results.despair: The one person who knows your real face is standing right there.

moves.name: Collateral
moves.trigger: When you put up something of value as surety to seal a deal:
moves.text: Take a boost die on rolls to uphold the deal while the collateral stands. If you forfeit it, mark XP and add 1 Heat.

moves.name: Eyes Everywhere
moves.trigger: When you spend a quiet day in a settlement, listening.

results.success: You learn a secret currently in play here.
results.failure: You hear only noise, and waste the day doing it.
advantage: Hold 1 Whisper (spend: reveal another live secret in this settlement).
You learn it without anyone knowing you were asking.
threat: Someone who matters heard you were asking: add 1 Heat.
results.triumph: You learn who is really pulling the strings in this place.
results.despair: You hear a secret someone will kill to keep, and they know you heard it.

hold.name: Whispers
spend: Reveal a secret currently in play in this settlement.

moves.name: The Patron's Purse
moves.text: Declare that your patron staked you. Gain coin or a needed common item now, and add 1 Debt.

moves.name: Hard to Kill
moves.trigger: When you would be taken out:
moves.text: You may add 1 Debt and stay standing one more exchange, visibly marked by how your patron reached out to save you.

bonds: ____ knew me before my patron did. They remember who I was.
____ has done work for me that could get them hanged, and I hold it gently.
I owe ____ a debt that has nothing to do with my patron, and it galls me.
____ thinks they can get me free of my patron. They are wrong, but I let them believe it.
____ is the only one I would warn before my patron came calling.
communityTie: Your patron wants a foothold in the home. ____, who lives there, does not know you are the wedge.
The home took you in when your patron once cast you off. You would protect ____ there even against orders.
You launder your patron's interests through ____'s business in the home.

tracks.name: Debt
tracks.note: At 6 the patron makes a major demand, then resets to 3.

basic: Take a move from another playbook.
Take a move from another playbook.
Gain a second patron (new Patron Sheet, Bond 0).
Increase your maximum held Strings by 1.
Train a move you lean on: it upgrades 2 dice to proficiency instead of 1 (never more than its stat).
Buy a piece of yourself back: reduce standing Debt by 1, permanently.
advanced: Raise one stat by 1 (max 5). Rare, and only at a true turning point.
Cut the Cord: end your bond with a patron for good, resolving the fallout in play. Choose a new embedment: claim or found a Bastion, or take up a god (start Devotion 1).
Become a Patron: create a small Patron Sheet with you as the gift-giver; others may now Invoke you.
